source_pr.title: session: make unit test TestDeadlock more stable
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->
Fixes https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/14680

### What is changed and how it works?
Assert either of the two transactions meets deadlock error instead of a certain one.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - No code

Related changes

 - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

symptoms: PR body: deadlock may be returned by either transaction; assert either of the two meets deadlock error instead of a certain one.
failure_signature: PR body: deadlock may be returned by either transaction; assert either of the two meets deadlock error instead of a certain one.
root_cause_explanation: The deadlock test relied on timing/scheduling and assumed a specific transaction would always observe the deadlock error. In practice, either transaction can hit the deadlock depending on execution order, making the assertion flaky. The PR stabilizes the test by using channel-based synchronization instead of `time.Sleep`, and by accepting the deadlock error from either side.
fix_pattern: Replace `time.Sleep` with channel synchronization and assert that either transaction returns `mysql.ErrLockDeadlock` (not a fixed side).

evidence: evidence: PR body: deadlock may be returned by either transaction; assert either of the two meets deadlock error instead of a certain one.
evidence: diff replaces time.Sleep + fixed-side assertion with channel-based synchronization and checks either err1 or err2 is mysql.ErrLockDeadlock (session/pessimistic_test.go).
